Event details

item.title: Songwriter Showcase w/Kristin Chambers\, Karen Laura Peters\, Allison Preisinger\, Fae Wiedenhoeft
item.description:
Saturday, March 28, 2026 at 7:30 PM

Location: Phinney Center Campus: Brick (lower) Building\, 6532 Phinney Ave. N.\, Seattle\, 98103\, United States

SFS presents an evening of music from 4 accomplished local songwriters! Kristin Chambers’ warm\, soulful style blends jazz\, pop\, and folk influences into a captivating sound. With a background in classical voice and a natural talent for crafting compelling narratives\, her music has found its way to stages and screens\, with placements on shows like “Better Call Saul\,” “The Young and the Restless\,” and “Love is Blind.” Her latest release\, “Kristin Chambers Select Songs Vol. 1\,” is a curated journey through her musical evolution as a songwriter thus far. Karen Laura Peters is a singing cellist with a collaborative nature and a gift for sharing her heart. While training at Oberlin Conservatory\, she fell in love with folk music and began writing her own songs\, joining a band (Her Treehouse) that was featured in NPR’s All Songs Considered blog in 2019. Peters is presently a member of the indie-soul band Queen of Hearts and co-founder of Momentum\, an organization that helps musicians feel inspired\, connected\, and empowered on their music journey. Allison Preisinger is a Berklee College of Music graduate known for evocative\, Americana-infused sounds and unique collaborations like her recent work on a project with the National Park Service. 2025 saw the release of her fourth album\, “Standing Steady\,” blending original music with poetry from a number of sources. A multi-instrumentalist with a passion for connection\, Preisinger uses art to uplift and share the beauty and complexity of human experience. Fae Wiedenhoeft is an award-winning songwriter and classically trained vocalist who was hired to play Celtic music in Canada at age 23 and has been a professional musician ever since. She tours regularly and has played in 10 countries solo and with her band\, SeaStar. Wiedenhoeft has recorded over two dozen albums and teaches voice\, guitar\, ukulele\, and songwriting. SeaStar’s latest album\, released in 2025\, is called “The In Between.” Doors open at 7 pm for the 7:30 pm concert in the Phinney Center’s Community Hall (lower Brick Building). Tickets are available in advance online\, in-person at prior SFS concerts\, or at the door.

From Phinney Neighborhood Association (PNA)

item.title: Spring Refresh featuring Screen Printing!
item.description:
Monday, March 30, 2026 at 5 PM

Location: Phinney Center Campus\, 6532 Phinney Ave. N.\, Seattle\, WA\, 98103\, United States

Spring Refresh: A Screen Printing Party and Clothing Drive  Come connect! Join us for Spring Refresh\, a hands-on event where neighbors come together to create\, give\, and care for one another. This evening is both a fun activity and an opportunity to show up for our community!  Screen Printing!  Bring a piece of light colored clothing to participate in screen-printing fun. PNA’s amazing Woodshop built two custom screen-printing presses for the night. (We will also have white t-shirts available for sale.) Choose from two designs:  The iconic PhinneyWood Heart – A bold\, graphic symbol of neighborhood pride and love for local small businesses. The PNA Orca\, designed by PNA Art Club member Chino – A powerful symbol of shared knowledge\, care across generations\, and community traditions. Let’s wear our values and tell the story of a community that shows up for one another. Spring Clothing Drive for the Hot Meal Program  As we gather\, we’ll also be collecting clean\, lightly worn clothing for adults to support the PNA’s Hot Meal Program Community Closet. Men’s clothing is especially needed. Some of our most requested items include: New socks and underwear Lightly used shoes and boots Men’s pants\, especially larger sizes Last year\, the Hot Meal Program served over 21\,000 free meals\, while also connecting neighbors to medical care\, haircuts\, and essential resources. Thanks to you\, this support is possible. When you bring clothing or donate\, you’re directly helping meet real needs in our neighborhood. Tunes\, Snacks & Lemonade  Enjoy music\, light snacks\, and refreshing lemonade while you print\, connect\, and give back. Who’s Invited  You! This all-ages event is open to neighbors\, PNA Members\, local PhinneyWood small business owners\, and anyone curious about the PNA. Come meet your neighbors\, learn what’s happening\, and be part of something meaningful. Event Details  Monday\, March 30 5:00–7:00 pm Phinney Center – Room 7 6532 Phinney Ave N\, Seattle\, WA 98103  Free for members  $10 for non-members Donations welcome to support the PNA programs such as the Hot Meal Program.  Spots are limited—register soon to reserve yours!   RSVP

From Phinney Neighborhood Association (PNA)

Children of all abilities are invited to this sensory friendly story time. Program in English and Spanish.-------------------------------------------------------Los niños de todas las habilidades están invitados a esta hora de cuentos sensorial. Evento en inglés y español. 

From SPL - Greenwood Branch

item.title: Ain't Misbehavin'
item.description:
Saturday, April 4, 2026 at 2 PM

Location: Taproot Theatre, 204 N 85th St, Seattle, WA 98103

The Fats Waller Music Show by Richard Maltby Jr. The Fats Waller Musical Show The club is alive, the band is swingin’, and the music of a jazz legend brings the house down. This immersive musical revue celebrates the life of Fats Waller on stage with joy, rhythm, and soul. Remembering his generation-defining songs and lasting impact on today’s music, this exuberant Tony Award-winning tribute to Black artistry sings, laughs, hopes, and lives out loud.

item.title: PETS: Park Orchard Elementary
item.description:
Tuesday, April 14, 2026 at 2:15 PM

Location: Taproot Theatre, 204 N 85th St, Seattle, WA 98103

Written By Melanie Godsey For grades K–5 | 1-hour presentation including performance and actor-led talkback It’s a tail-wagging time at Pet School, where Biscuit the pup and all their furry and feathered friends learn to sit, stay, and help a human in need. When things get ruff for Biscuit’s best human friend, Corey, it’s time to fetch help. A once-close friendship has gone to the dogs, and the bus stop becomes a bullying zone. Using every paws-itively clever trick from Pet School, Biscuit will stop at nothing to help Corey and teach the other kids that being an upstander is the best trick of all. Education Goals: Bullying prevention Turning bystanders into upstanders Overcoming failure Teamwork

From Taproot Theatre

Event details

item.title: Barefoot in the Park
item.description:
Wednesday, May 13, 2026 at 7:30 PM

Location: Taproot Theatre, 204 N 85th St, Seattle, WA 98103

By Neil Simon They’ve just said, “I do,” but can their love handle five flights of stairs and a tiny New York apartment? A buttoned-up young lawyer and his free-spirited bride stumble into married life, eccentric neighbors, leaky ceilings, and romantic chaos. Full of wit, chemistry, and charm, this classic rom-com bubbles over with laughter and warmth. Love is messy—and that's what makes it fun.

From Taproot Theatre

item.title: Barefoot in the Park
item.description:
Wednesday, June 3, 2026 at 2 PM

Location: Taproot Theatre, 204 N 85th St, Seattle, WA 98103

By Neil Simon They’ve just said, “I do,” but can their love handle five flights of stairs and a tiny New York apartment? A buttoned-up young lawyer and his free-spirited bride stumble into married life, eccentric neighbors, leaky ceilings, and romantic chaos. Full of wit, chemistry, and charm, this classic rom-com bubbles over with laughter and warmth. Love is messy—and that's what makes it fun.

From Taproot Theatre
